description: Bewick's Wren (Thryomanes bewickii): Medium-sized wren with rather long white eyebrow. Longish, slightly decurved bill. Upper mandible blackish, lower mandible lighter gray. Fairly long tail. Feathers under the tail barred, some with white spots. Upperparts grayish-brown, throat whitish, underparts whitish, gray on sides, flanks and legs.Behaviors: Moved rapidly among shrubs and bushes on the hillside. Waged tail from side to side.Call: not heardPlumage: adult
